title: Southbound Crude Transits Via Bab El Mandeb Ease On Rising Security Risk
section_path: Southbound Crude Transits Via Bab El Mandeb Ease On Rising Security Risk
section_path_text: Southbound Crude Transits Via Bab El Mandeb Ease On Rising Security Risk
summary:
Main
July saw a dip in southbound crude transits via Bab-el-Mandeb as renewed Red Sea attacks, and growing compliance pressures prompted selective rerouting and owner caution. By Wanying Zhang

Renewed attacks and softer demand trim BEM crude flows
On 6 and 7 July, Houthi forces launched renewed attacks in the Red Sea, this time striking bulk carriers. While tankers were not directly impacted, the incidents have reignited security concerns among shipowners operating in the region-particularly those with exposure to Bab-el-Mandeb (BEM) transits.

section_path: Renewed attacks and softer demand trim BEM crude flows
children.section_path_text: Renewed attacks and softer demand trim BEM crude flows
children.summary: On 6 and 7 July, Houthi forces launched renewed attacks in the Red Sea, this time striking bulk carriers. While tankers were not directly impacted, the incidents have reignited security concerns among shipowners operating in the region-particularly those with exposure to Bab-el-Mandeb (BEM) transits.

children.title: Strategic Pullback in Greek Participation
children.summary: Weekly voyage counts by Greek operators in moving Russian crude via BEM peaked in Q2 2023-coinciding with a period when Urals was trading below the G7 price cap. A similar uptick occurred in January 2025, when prices once again fell below the cap and a new wave of OFAC sanctions was introduced.

children.title: BEM Corridor Open, But Confidence Fading
section_path: BEM Corridor Open, But Confidence Fading
children.section_path_text: BEM Corridor Open, But Confidence Fading
children.summary: Bab-el-Mandeb remains the main conduit for southbound crude flows, with overall transit volumes holding steady. The recent dip in flows is primarily a function of weaker Indian demand, driven by seasonal refinery maintenance in the short-term future could come due to a reduction in purchases by private refiners such as Nayara Energy.
